ticket_contract.work_type: Internal Draft Workflow / Client Projection Safety
ticket_contract.unique_shape: Fail-closed internal-only ticket; its core acceptance is absence of client leakage, not visible feature richness.
ticket_contract.overclaim_guard: Analyst-visible drafts must remain invisible to clients until compliance release.

canonical_storage.purpose: Owns analyst/internal draft text, internal rationale, source context, process id, draft status and current trace pointer.
Records internal classification, risk level and unsupported-claim clearance decision.
Records claim key, evidence requirement, source reference and blocker status.
Connects draft events to audit rows and accepted evidence records.
canonical_storage.client_projection: client sees only absence of trace availability

legacy_fallback_position.decision: No new draft workflow may write overloaded Recommendation.assumptionsJson or Recommendation.clientSummaryDraft fields.
legacy_fallback_position.reason: Drafts need a first-class internal spine so client projection cannot accidentally inherit internal text from the recommendation row.
legacy_fallback_position.proof: tests/internal-draft-governance-spine.spec.ts scans production recommendation mutations for overloaded draft-field writes.

non_bypass_rules: Advisor handoff requires classification plus evidence-backed rebuild when unsupported claims existed.
Rejected drafts cannot move to advisor, compliance, release, export or client visibility.
Admin governance cannot force draft clearance, evidence sufficiency, release, export or internal payload visibility.
Client-safe projection cannot expose internal draft trace, internal rationale, unsupported claim detail or source refs.

source_evidence.role: Defines InternalDraft, DraftClassification, UnsupportedClaim and DraftTrace as first-class models.
Implements draft creation, classification, unsupported claim persistence, rejection, rebuild, trace map, negative gate matrix and exit certification.
Proves first-class models and forbids legacy overloaded Recommendation-field fallback.
Proves the ordered internal draft workflow, including negative gates.

acceptance_mapping.analyst_rebuild_contract: satisfied through accepted scoped evidence requirement and DraftTrace evidence linkage
acceptance_mapping.absence_of_client_feature_claim: satisfied; this subtask does not claim visible feature richness

commands_run.command: pnpm playwright test tests/internal-draft-governance-spine.spec.ts tests/p44-phase5-certification.spec.ts tests/client-visibility-projection.spec.ts tests/true-ux-p0-safety.spec.ts tests/export-safety.spec.ts --workers=1

validation.positive_acceptance_result: Internal drafts have a canonical internal spine and evidence-backed rebuild path.
validation.negative_acceptance_result: Legacy Recommendation-field fallback, unclassified handoff, unsupported-claim handoff and rejected-draft progress are blocked by tests.

bold_cleanup_recommendations: Deprecate Recommendation.clientSummaryDraft and Recommendation.assumptionsJson for new internal draft workflows instead of keeping them as silent compatibility surfaces.
Make InternalDraft the only accepted write target for analyst draft content, model prompts, source refs and internal rationale.
Promote DraftTrace to the canonical audit/evidence map for internal draft proof and forbid UI components from reconstructing this trace from display payloads.
remaining_limits: The Recommendation table still contains historical draft-like nullable fields; BD-06 locks non-use for new production writes but does not migrate old columns away.
This subtask did not add a new UI state machine screen because the ticket's acceptance is confidentiality and negative proof.
